paper_title: Method of Transformations of Geraniol and Analyses of the Post-Reaction Mixtures

methods.description: Chemical synthesis of geraniol transformations carried out in a glass reactor under controlled temperature, catalyst content, and reaction time.

steps.description: The syntheses were carried out in a glass reactor with a capacity of 25 cm 3 , which was equipped with a reflux condenser and a magnetic stirrer with heating function.
steps.searchable_summary: Set up chemical reactor for synthesis

steps.description: The ranges of the studied parameters were as follows: temperature 80-150 • C, catalyst content 5-15 wt %, reaction time from 15 min to 24 h.
steps.searchable_summary: Run chemical reaction under specified conditions

methods.description: Preparation steps for the post-reaction mixture sample before chromatographic analysis, involving centrifugation and dissolution.

steps.description: In order to perform a qualitative and quantitative analyses, the sample of the post-reaction mixture was first centrifuged...
...and then it was dissolved in acetone in the ratio 1:3.

methods.description: Identification of compounds in the prepared post-reaction mixture sample using Gas Chromatography-Mass Spectrometry (GC-MS).

steps.description: Qualitative analyses were performed using the GC-MS method on a ThermoQuest apparatus with a Voyager detector and a DB-5 column (filled with phenylmethylsiloxanes, 30 m × 0.25 mm × 0.5 mm).
inputs: GC-MS apparatus (ThermoQuest, Voyager detector, DB-5 column)

steps.description: Analysis parameters: helium flow 1 mL/min, sample chamber temperature 200 • C, detector temperature 250 • C, oven temperature-isothermally for 2.5 min at 50 • C, then heating at the rate of 10 • C/min to 300 • C.
inputs: analysis parameters (helium flow, temperatures, oven program)

methods.description: Quantification of compounds in the prepared post-reaction mixture sample using Gas Chromatography with Flame Ionization Detector (GC-FID).

steps.description: Quantitative analyses were performed with help of Thermo Electron FOCUS chromatograph with FID detector and TR-FAME column (cyanopropylphenyl packed, 30 m × 0.25 mm × 0.25 mm).
inputs: GC-FID apparatus (Thermo Electron FOCUS, FID detector, TR-FAME column)

steps.description: The analysis parameters were as follows: helium flow 0.7 mL/min, sample chamber temperature 200 • C, detector temperature 250 • C, oven temperature-isothermally for 7 min at 60 • C, then heating at the rate of 15 • C/min to 240 • C. The FID temperature was kept at the level of 250 • C.
inputs: analysis parameters (helium flow, temperatures, oven program)

methods.method_name: Product Quantification and Process Metrics Calculation
methods.description: Methods used to quantify the amounts of products and reactants from chromatographic data and calculate key process performance indicators like selectivity and conversion.

steps.description: The quantitative analyses of the products were performed using the external and internal standard method.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply external or internal standard method for quantification

steps.description: In case of the first method, 8-point calibration curves were performed for each compound in the concentration range of 0-33 wt %.
steps.searchable_summary: Generate calibration curves for external standard method
inputs: standard samples with known concentrations (0-33 wt%)

steps.description: After the chromatographic analyses the mass balances for each synthesis were prepared.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare mass balances for each synthesis

steps.description: The mass balances allowed us to calculate the main functions describing the process: S product/geraniol = Amount of moles of product Amount of moles of geraniol consumed × 100%
inputs: amount of moles of geraniol consumed

steps.description: (1) C geraniol = Amount of moles of geraniol consumed Amount of moles of geraniol introduced into reactor × 100%
inputs: amount of moles of geraniol consumed
amount of moles of geraniol introduced
